reviewTarget: Audit the shipped /goal OpenCode plugin implementation in packet deep-loops/032-goal-opencode-plugin (phases 001-state-store through 008-system-spec-kit-integration only; EXCLUDE phase 009-speckit-command-goal-prompt-offer, which is actively owned by a separate in-flight OpenCode session). Review dimensions: correctness (does .opencode/plugins/mk-goal.js match its own phase spec/plan claims), security (prompt-injection sanitization, secret redaction in evidence/logs), spec-alignment/traceability (drift between the 8 phase docs and the live .opencode/plugins/mk-goal.js / .opencode/commands/opencode_goal.md / test suite), completeness/maintainability (missing test coverage, dead code, UX gaps, automation/integration gaps).

focus: correctness -- inventory + pass A: state-store + injection-plugin

findingDetails.title: Injection max-length option does not cap the rendered active-goal block
findingDetails.claim: Phase 002 claims renderGoalInjection caps output length, but implementation can return a block longer than maxInjectionChars.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched tests for maxInjectionChars assertions and found no total block-length assertion.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The option might have been intended as a prompt-subsection budget only, but phase docs say block/output length.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if docs are amended to define maxInjectionChars as a soft prompt-subsection budget.
findingDetails.recommendation: Clamp or budget the final rendered block and add a total-length assertion.

findingDetails.title: Replacement goals can be continued after a stale verifier result was intentionally discarded
findingDetails.claim: A replacement goal can be actively continued after a stale verifier result was intentionally not applied.

findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Runtime-level serialization may prevent interleaving goal replacement while supervisorVerifier is awaited, but that invariant is not encoded here.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Prove runtime serialization prevents this interleaving, or add same-goal/applied-result gating before continuation.

findingDetails.title: Active-goal sanitization is a narrow blacklist before promoting user objective text into system context
findingDetails.claim: A malicious objective can still carry system-prompt-like instructions into the injected active-goal block because the sanitizer only strips a few ASCII role-prefix and instruction-reset patterns before renderGoalInjection promotes the derived goalPrompt into output.system.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked set/replacement, stored normalization, enhancement, injection, and current adversarial tests; all use the same narrow sanitizer and tests cover marker breakout, raw ASCII role labels, two instruction-reset phrase families, and triple backticks only.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The implementation may intentionally treat the active goal as user-authored guidance, but the plugin persists that text and reinjects it as system context, making the sanitizer the protection against durable instruction elevation.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the active-goal injection contract is explicitly changed to say user objectives are trusted instructions by design and only marker/format breakout is in scope.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact search and direct reads covered sanitizeInlineText, sanitizePromptText, buildEnhancedGoalPrompt, normalizeStoredGoal, setGoal, renderGoalInjection, appendGoalBrief, executeGoalAction, mk-goal-state adversarial tests, and mk-goal-supervisor evidence redaction tests.

searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Sanitization occurs before and during deterministic prompt generation.

focus: security -- PASS B: secret redaction in evidence/logs and state persistence safety

findingDetails.title: Verifier exception messages bypass secret redaction before persistence and injection/status output
findingDetails.claim: A secret-bearing supervisor-verifier exception message is sanitized but not redacted, then persisted as lastVerifierReason and surfaced through injection_preview in status/chat output.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Reviewed normal verifier evidence normalization and supervisor tests. Normal rawResult.evidence and fallback evidence are redacted through redactEvidence, and tests assert API key plus sk-... samples do not remain in stored evidence or status output. No equivalent code path or test redacts error.message before it becomes lastVerifierReason.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: If every configured supervisorVerifier implementation is fully trusted and guaranteed never to throw secret-bearing messages, this becomes a hardening gap rather than an active leak. That guarantee is not enforced at the plugin boundary.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 only if the verifier boundary is constrained so exception messages cannot contain user, provider, transport, credential, or connection-string data, and that constraint is covered by tests.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact read of all redactEvidence, lastVerifierReason, renderGoalInjection, goalStateLines, and failure/error paths found one unredacted exception-to-status/injection route; debug and continuation logs do not write evidence/objective text.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: non-trivial security review of plugin state persistence, redaction, status, injection, and logging paths

targetSelection.selectionReason: Prompt-named security pass B targets for redaction helpers, verifier/status output, logs, and state path safety.

lastVerifierReason is included in renderGoalInjection and emitted through injection_preview

searchLedger.rationale: Redaction helper coverage exists but is bypassed by the verifier exception path.

tests cover api_key and sk-... redaction in stored evidence and status output

searchLedger.rationale: The normal evidence path applies redactEvidence and has positive coverage for representative secrets.

focus: traceability -- PASS A: core protocols consolidated sweep across phases 001-008

findingDetails.title: Command surface traceability has split into three incompatible names
findingDetails.claim: The eight scoped phase docs and review packet do not identify a single live command artifact for the goal command surface: phase docs and metadata name goal.md, prior review state names opencode_goal.md, and the current live file is goal_opencode.md while its heading remains # /goal.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked command directory for goal.md, opencode_goal.md, and goal_opencode.md; only goal_opencode.md exists. Reviewed known DR-002 and treated this as a broader cross-phase traceability split rather than re-reporting the existing bare-/goal naming drift.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: An external command alias might map /goal or /opencode_goal to goal_opencode.md, but no in-scope phase metadata documents that alias.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if an in-repo OpenCode command alias manifest proves all three names resolve to the same executable command and phase metadata intentionally preserves historical names.

findingDetails.title: Generated phase graph metadata includes non-deliverable files as key phase outputs
findingDetails.claim: Some phase-level graph-metadata.json files do not accurately describe the phase deliverables listed in that phase's tasks and implementation summary.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked that named files exist in the repository; existence alone does not make them phase deliverables. Compared metadata key_files to same-phase tasks and implementation-summary Files Changed tables.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The metadata generator may include mentioned/contextual files, not only deliverables; if intended, the field name key_files is misleading for phase deliverable traceability.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if graph metadata's contract explicitly allows non-deliverable contextual files in derived.key_files and downstream memory consumers do not treat them as phase-owned outputs.

findingDetails.claim: The overlay feature catalogs and manual testing playbooks still present .opencode/commands/goal.md and /goal as the operator command surface, but the current live command file found by a fresh glob is .opencode/commands/goal_opencode.md.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Fresh glob of .opencode/commands/*goal* returned only .opencode/commands/goal_opencode.md; precise agent-surface greps for mk-goal|mk_goal|goal_opencode|opencode_goal|active_goal|/goal under .opencode/agents and .claude/agents found no files; plugin tool schemas still expose mk_goal and mk_goal_status at .opencode/plugins/mk-goal.js:1625.

findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 or close if runtime evidence proves /goal remains a supported alias for .opencode/commands/goal_opencode.md and the overlay docs are updated to cite the live command file path.

focus: maintainability -- PASS A: test coverage gaps, dead code, patterns

findingDetails.title: Current plugin tests do not pin the regression cases for four active behavior/security findings
findingDetails.claim: The current plugin-test suite has only partial or adjacent assertions for DR-001-P1-001, DR-003-P1-001, DR-005-P1-001, and DR-006-P1-001; it would not reliably fail if those fixes regressed in the specific ways already reported.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched all six mk-goal tests for maxInjectionChars, renderGoalInjection, semantic injection patterns, Verifier failed, and maybeContinueGoal; reviewed adjacent tests and found no exact whole-block length, thrown-exception secret, or stale-verifier-race regression.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Some gaps are expected because the underlying defects are still open, but without these tests a remediation can appear green while reintroducing the same behavior.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if focused regression tests are added for the four behaviors or the affected code paths are removed instead of fixed.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The six scoped test files cover state, lifecycle, supervisor, continuation, tool-path, and export contract, but not the exact regression scenarios.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add focused regression assertions for whole injection block cap, semantic sanitizer bypass, verifier exception secret redaction, and stale verifier replacement/continuation integration.

findingDetails.title: Prompt-enhancement tests lock CRAFT and DEPTH but not the required RICCE metadata contract
findingDetails.claim: DR-004-P1-001 would not be caught by current tests because they assert CRAFT+TIDD-EC, DEPTH, CLEAR score, and prompt length but never assert RICCE metadata.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched the plugin tests for RICCE/ricce and reviewed prompt metadata assertions in state and tool-path tests.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: RICCE could be amended to documentation-only, but the active finding states stored prompt metadata must name RICCE.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the phase 007 acceptance criterion is amended or tests assert the agreed RICCE metadata shape.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Only state and tool-path tests assert prompt enhancement metadata, and neither names RICCE.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add a prompt-enhancement metadata regression test for the required RICCE contract.

findingDetails.title: Command and overlay documentation drift has no regression harness in the current test suite
findingDetails.claim: The mk-goal test suite imports only the plugin module and never validates command markdown, command filename normalization, feature catalogs, or manual-testing playbooks, so DR-002-P1-001, DR-007-P1-001, DR-008-P1-001, and related unknown-verb drift can recur without a scoped test failing.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched all six plugin tests for command names and stale command-surface terms; no command-doc/catalog/playbook validation appeared.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: A validator may exist outside the scoped mk-goal test suite, but it is not represented in the six scoped test files named for this pass.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if an existing CI-wired validator checks command markdown names and catalog/playbook command references.
findingDetails.scopeProof: No scoped test file corresponds to command markdown or overlay catalog/playbook surfaces.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add a lightweight command/overlay contract test or validator that asserts one canonical command surface and expected unknown-verb behavior.

findingDetails.title: Graph-metadata deliverable drift is not covered by a phase metadata regression check
findingDetails.scopeProof: No scoped test file corresponds to phase graph-metadata validation.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add or cite a metadata-shape validator for phase key files and non-deliverable outputs.

focus: maintainability -- PASS B: UX gaps and automation/integration gaps

findingDetails.title: MK_GOAL_PLUGIN_DISABLED documentation overstates the disable boundary
findingDetails.claim: The documented disable flag says it disables goal injection and goal plugin behavior, but the implementation only suppresses passive injection and autonomous continuation while mk_goal tool mutations still execute.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked the live command router and plugin executor for an alternate disabled-mode guard; command routes directly to mk_goal/mk_goal_status and executeGoalAction has no options.enabled guard before mutations.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The intended contract may be to disable passive injection and autonomous continuation only while leaving manual tools active; if so, documentation should be narrowed.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the intended public contract is explicitly injection/autonomy-only and ENV_REFERENCE is updated to say manual tool commands remain available.

findingDetails.title: /goal set output does not tell users whether it created, replaced, or refreshed a goal
findingDetails.recommendation: Add a terse mutation-result field such as mutation=created|replaced|refreshed and optionally previous_goal_id for replacements.

traceabilityChecks.spec_code: partial: MK_GOAL_* variables are documented, but MK_GOAL_PLUGIN_DISABLED wording overstates the actual tool disable boundary

traceabilityChecks.automation: partial: existing hooks do not cover goal command/env/RICCE drift

findingDetails.title: Findings registry drops claim-adjudication fields for active findings
findingDetails.claim: The findings registry cannot satisfy the final-pass claim-adjudication contract because openFindings entries omit claim, evidenceRefs, counterevidenceSought, alternativeExplanation, finalSeverity, confidence, and downgradeTrigger for all 16 active findings.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched the registry for required field names and parsed registry openFindings plus state-log findingDetails. P1 state-log details retain adjudication fields, but registry openFindings do not; three P2 state-log details also lack the full field set.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The reducer may intentionally keep the registry shallow and rely on JSONL state records for full adjudication, but this iteration explicitly requested registry consistency for all active findings before synthesis.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 or close if the reducer contract is clarified that the registry is intentionally shallow and synthesis always joins against deep-review-state.jsonl, or if active registry entries are enriched before synthesis.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Parsed all 16 active registry entries and all were missing the seven requested fields; parsed state-log details to distinguish registry projection loss from original finding-detail absence.
findingDetails.recommendation: Before synthesis, enrich active registry entries with claim-adjudication fields or make the synthesis join against state-log findingDetails explicit and validated.
